title: Document TUI Package Layout Changes
description: Update any developer-facing documentation that references `src/agent_takt/tui.py` or `tests/test_tui.py`, or explicitly confirm that no repository docs required updates because the refactor preserved the external API.
acceptance_criteria: Any repository documentation that names the old monolithic TUI module or test file is updated to the package layout.
If no such references exist, the bead records that no documentation changes were necessary.
Documentation continues to describe the preserved `run_tui` import surface accurately.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated three documentation files: CLAUDE.md (project layout), docs/development.md (project layout + testing section), and docs/tui.md (EXECUTION_HISTORY_DISPLAY_LIMIT source reference). All stale references to tui.py and test_tui.py in developer-facing docs are resolved.
handoff_summary.risks: None. Changes are purely documentary; no runtime behaviour was altered.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. Only documentation files were modified. No overlap with sibling beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: EXECUTION_HISTORY_DISPLAY_LIMIT source reference updated from tui.py to tui/state.py (where it is now defined). The constant is also re-exported from tui/__init__.py so the public import surface is unchanged. The test_cli_tui.py reference in docs/development.md was intentionally left — it tests the CLI takt tui command, not TUI internals, and the file still exists.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No test changes. This bead is documentation-only.

conflict_risks: None. Only documentation files were modified. No overlap with sibling beads.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.

worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks:
Beads that claim `src/agent_takt/tui.py` as an expected file must be aware it no longer exists; the file is now `src/agent_takt/tui/__init__.py`.
None — only tui package files were modified. No overlap with other in-progress beads expected.
No file-scope conflicts with sibling beads. tree.py is a new file with no prior claims. state.py edits are contained to the import block and removed symbol definitions.
Low. Changes are pure structural moves within the tui package. No logic was altered — only function locations. All external import paths are preserved via re-exports in `__init__.py`.
src/agent_takt/tui/state.py and src/agent_takt/tui/actions.py were modified/created by sibling beads in the same feature tree. These do not conflict with test file edits.
Low. actions.py is a new file. state.py changes are internal (method bodies replaced by delegation). render.py and __init__.py are untouched. No expected_files overlaps with sibling beads.
No file-scope conflicts expected. `app.py` is a new file; `__init__.py` only has symbol re-exports changed.
None. This bead only reorganizes test files — no production code was touched.
None. The deleted `tests/test_tui.py` was only referenced by spec/bead metadata (historical) and not imported by any other test or source module.
